quotes.text: You would not be able to perceive a sufficient amount of data to make any deductions. This is not to say that such planes or fields of actuality cannot in some manner be perceived, but they will escape physical perception or exploration. In dreams, when the self is somewhat free from camouflage, perhaps it is possible for the self then to travel spontaneously into such fields of actuality; but since they are as bizarre to the self as is the dream world itself, then there is no way, usually, for the sleeper to distinguish between the universe of dreams or other actualities.
quotes.translation: 你无法感知足够的数据来做出任何推论。这并非说这些实相层面或场域无法以某种方式被感知，但它们将避开物质性的感知或探索。在梦境中，当自我稍微脱离伪装时，或许自我能自发地进入这类实相场域；但由于它们对自我而言与梦境世界本身同样奇异，因此通常沉睡者无法区分梦境宇宙或其他实相。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: And in the same way that the dream world has no beginning or end, neither does the universe with which you are familiar. No energy can be withdrawn, and this includes the energy used in the continuous subconscious construction of the dream world. You continually create it, have always created it. It is a product of your own existence, and you can neither consciously call it into existence nor destroy it.
quotes.translation: 正如梦境世界没有起点或终点，你所熟悉的宇宙也是如此。没有任何能量可以被撤回，这包括用于持续无意识地构建梦境世界的能量。你不断地创造它，一直创造着它。它是你自身存在的产物，你既无法有意识地召唤它存在，也无法摧毁它。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: The human system then translates the experience, but its original existence and actuality is electrical. This is why your dream locations take up no physical space, either within your skull or in your physical universe; and yet I have said that these dream locations did exist. Their existence is electrical, and they partake of the dimensions of the electrical system as I have given them to you.
quotes.translation: 人类系统随后会转译这种经验，但其原始存在与实相是电性的。这就是为何你的梦境地点不占据任何物质空间——无论在你的头颅内还是物质宇宙中；然而我曾说过这些梦境地点确实存在。它们的存有是电性的，并且如我所告知的，它们参与了电性系统的维度。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: The dream world and the mind are touched by time, and exist in it only in so far as these realities dip into the camouflage universe. Basically both the dream world and the ideas of the inner mind do not have their existence in time, although they may be visible from the perspective of time, viewed from the physical form.
quotes.translation: 梦境世界与心灵受到时间触及，但仅当这些实相涉入伪装宇宙时，才存在于时间之中。从根本上说，梦境世界与内在心灵的理念并不存在于时间之内，尽管从物质形态的角度、透过时间的视角，它们或许能被瞥见。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: If the dream world and the mind and the inner universe do exist, and if they do not exist in space, and if they do not exist basically in time, though they may be glimpsed through time, then your question will be: in what medium or in what manner do they exist; and without time how can they be said to exist in duration?
quotes.translation: 倘若梦境世界、心灵与内在宇宙确实存在，且它们不存在于空间之中，又基本上不存在于时间之内——尽管可能透过时间被隐约感知——那么你的问题将是：它们以何种媒介或何种方式存在？若无时间，又怎能说它们存在于绵延之中？
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: The ascension of Christ did not occur in time as you know it. It is a contribution of the universe of dreams to your own universe, representing knowledge within the dream universe that man was independent of physical matter ultimately.
quotes.translation: 基督的升天并未发生在你所认知的时间中。它是梦境宇宙对你自身宇宙的贡献，代表了梦境宇宙中的知识：人类最终独立于物质形体。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: Such developments would mean the releasing and availability of added energy into your field, that would have endless possibilities in all directions. Ideas and concepts are nonphysical actualities that attract unaligned energy, direct and concentrate it. The dream world exists more closely in that spacious present of which the inner self is so aware. It is not as involved with camouflage constructions as your own universe, and its actions are somewhat limited within its own framework.
quotes.translation: 此类发展意味着将释放并启用额外的能量进入你们的场域，这些能量在所有方向上都拥有无限可能性。观念与概念是非物质的实相，它们吸引未定向的能量，引导并凝聚之。梦境世界更贴近于内在自我所深刻觉察的那个广阔当下。它不像你们的宇宙那样深陷于伪装建构之中，其活动在其自身框架内也受到一定限制。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: In this chapter we will be concerned with the effect that dream experiences and objective experiences have upon each other. Certainly there is a connection... in terms of events... between waking and dreaming reality as we know it. If rauixuxx our dreams effect our objective actions in any way at all, on an individual then we as a race are 'also effected, en masse, in the physical universe, by our collective dreams, dreams could then be seen as a causal element in physical events.
quotes.translation: 在本章中，我们将探讨梦境体验与客观体验如何相互影响。诚然，就事件层面而言，清醒现实与梦境现实之间存在着关联。如果我们的梦境以任何方式影响个体的客观行为，那么作为种族，我们全体也必然在物质宇宙中受到集体梦境的影响。由此，梦境可被视为物质事件中的因果要素。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)
quotes.source_file: ES-6-The Early Sessions-Book 6 of The Seth Material(240-280).json

quotes.text: We have left many loose ends since our last session. I have told you that it is possible to tune in on the dream universe. You know this is done during sleep. It can also be done in the so-called waking state.
quotes.translation: 自上次课程以来，我们遗留了许多未解之题。我曾告诉你们，调频至梦境宇宙是可能的。你们知道这发生在睡眠期间，但同样也能在所谓的清醒状态下实现。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: In the dream universe however the slower physical growth process is replaced by psychic and mental value fulfillment, which does not necessitate any long-range imprisonment of molecules within a pattern.
quotes.translation: 然而在梦的宇宙里，较慢的肉体成长过程被心灵与心智的价值完成所取代，这不需要将分子长期囚禁在某种模式中。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: The conscious mind does not even know, and cannot of itself command, the legs to walk across the floor. Is it any wonder, then, that the conscious mind does not know how it creates the dream universe?
quotes.translation: 意识心甚至不知道，也无法自行命令双腿走过地板。那么，意识心不知道它如何创造梦的宇宙，又有什么好奇怪的呢？
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: "If we really understand how dreams worked and allowed ourselves to explore dream levels, we'd see how the universe is formed. It is the ... creative product, en masse, of our individual and joint dreams ... Our world is a dream level for some other types of consciousness; it's shared to some extent, then, and can serve as a meeting point.
quotes.translation: "如果我们真正了解梦如何运作，并允许自己探索梦的层次，我们就会看到宇宙是如何形成的。它是……我们个人与集体梦境的创造性产物……我们的世界是其他某种意识的一个梦的层次；它在某种程度上是共享的，因此可以作为一个会合点。"
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The material of the physical universe is created spontaneously and constantly, even as the dream locations in the dream world are so created; and as it is impossible in terms of time as you know it to set a point of beginning in the dream world, so it is impossible to attempt to do the same as far as the physical universe is concerned.
quotes.translation: 物质宇宙的材料是自发且持续创造的，正如梦境世界中的地点也是如此；而就你所知的时间而言，无法在梦境世界中设定一个起点，因此就物质宇宙而言，也无法尝试做同样的事。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: Both the dream universe and the universe of negative matter then, are by-products of the physical universe, in that they were formed as inward energy attempted to form itself in a physical way.
quotes.translation: 梦境宇宙和负物质宇宙都是物质宇宙的副产品，因为它们是内在能量试图以物质方式形成自身时产生的。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: Dream images are indeed, in density, between the tangible nature of physical objects and the intangible reality that is entirely independent of physical matter. The dream reality cannot be entirely disconnected from the material universe because of its connections with human personality. There is a dependence here.
quotes.translation: 梦境影像的密度确实介于物质实体的可触本质与完全独立于物质实体的不可触实相之间。梦境实相无法完全脱离物质宇宙，因为它与人类人格相连。这里存在一种依赖关系。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: You do this in the dream state. You do it in the waking state. There must be therefore definite connections between inner energy and the world of objects. There are methods and means, hidden to the ego, whereby the physical universe as you know it is constantly created and maintained. Underline as you know it.
quotes.translation: 你在梦境状态中这样做。你在清醒状态中也这样做。因此，内在能量与物体世界之间必然存在明确的联系。有些方法和手段是自我所隐藏的，你们所知的物质宇宙正是通过这些方法和手段被不断创造和维持的。请强调“你们所知的”。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: So, when you consider the dream world, you have the same sort of a universe, only one constructed on or within a field which your outer senses cannot perceive. But it has more continuity than the world known by the outer senses intimately, and there are similarities within it that are amazing to behold.
quotes.translation: 所以，当你考虑梦境世界时，你拥有同样类型的宇宙，只是它构建在一个你们外在感官无法感知的场域或场域之内。但它比外在感官所熟知的世界具有更强的连续性，并且其中存在着令人惊叹的相似性。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: The question however, in what dimensions do dream locations exist, was simply based. Nevertheless it is one that has many implications, for which you need more background. I will attempt a simplified explanation then. Do you remember some of the material that I gave concerning the expanding universe?
quotes.translation: 然而，关于“梦境地点存在于什么维度”这个问题，其基础很简单。尽管如此，它却具有许多含义，为此你需要更多的背景知识。那么我将尝试做一个简化的解释。你还记得我给出的关于膨胀宇宙的一些资料吗？
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)
quotes.source_file: ES-6-The Early Sessions-Book 6 of The Seth Material(240-280).json

quotes.text: It is formed equally by those experiences which are purely subjective, and which exist only within the psychological time framework. The subjective experiences therefore result in definite changes within the physical body framework. These changes are not caused because of a physical event, but because of an event occurring within a dream condition which has no reality in your physical universe.
quotes.translation: 它同样由那些纯主观的体验所形成，这些体验只存在于心理时间框架内。因此，主观体验会在物质身体框架内引发明确的变化。这些变化并非由物质事件引起，而是源于梦境状态中发生的事件——该事件在你们的物质宇宙中并无实相。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: As the dream does not begin then at any particular moment, neither did your physical universe begin at any given moment, and neither did the inwardness become born in terms of cause and effect.
quotes.translation: 正如梦境并非始于某个特定时刻，你们的物质宇宙也非始于任何既定时刻，而内在性也非以因果关系的模式诞生。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
